    80	export const getPublicKey = (
    81	  privateKey: Uint8Array | string,
    82	  isCompressed: boolean = true,
    83	): Uint8Array => {
    84	  return p256.getPublicKey(privateKey, isCompressed);
    85	};
    86	
    87	/**
    88	 * HPKE Encrypt Function
    89	 * Encrypts data using Hybrid Public Key Encryption (HPKE) standard https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/rfc9180/.
    90	 *
    91	 * @param {HpkeEncryptParams} params - The encryption parameters including plain text, encapsulated key, and sender private key.
    92	 * @returns {Uint8Array} - The encrypted data.
    93	 */
    94	
    95	export const hpkeEncrypt = ({
    96	  plainTextBuf,
    97	  targetKeyBuf,
    98	}: HpkeEncryptParams): Uint8Array => {
    99	  try {
   100	    // Standard HPKE Mode (Ephemeral Key Pair)
   101	    const ephemeralKeyPair = generateP256KeyPair();
   102	    const senderPrivBuf = uint8ArrayFromHexString(ephemeralKeyPair.privateKey);
   103	    const senderPubBuf = uint8ArrayFromHexString(
   104	      ephemeralKeyPair.publicKeyUncompressed,
   105	    );
   106	
   107	    const aad = buildAdditionalAssociatedData(senderPubBuf, targetKeyBuf);
   108	
   109	    // Step 1: Generate Shared Secret
   110	    const ss = deriveSS(targetKeyBuf, uint8ArrayToHexString(senderPrivBuf!));
   111	
   112	    // Step 2: Generate the KEM context
   113	    const kemContext = getKemContext(
   114	      senderPubBuf,
   115	      uint8ArrayToHexString(targetKeyBuf),
   116	    );
   117	
   118	    // Step 3: Build the HKDF inputs for key derivation
   119	    let ikm = buildLabeledIkm(LABEL_EAE_PRK, ss, SUITE_ID_1);
   120	    let info = buildLabeledInfo(
   121	      LABEL_SHARED_SECRET,
   122	      kemContext,
   123	      SUITE_ID_1,
   124	      32,
   125	    );
   126	    const sharedSecret = extractAndExpand(new Uint8Array([]), ikm, info, 32);
   127	
   128	    // Step 4: Derive the AES key
   129	    ikm = buildLabeledIkm(LABEL_SECRET, new Uint8Array([]), SUITE_ID_2);
   130	    info = AES_KEY_INFO;
   131	    const key = extractAndExpand(sharedSecret, ikm, info, 32);
   132	
   133	    // Step 5: Derive the initialization vector
   134	    info = IV_INFO;
   135	    const iv = extractAndExpand(sharedSecret, ikm, info, 12);
   136	
   137	    // Step 6: Encrypt the data using AES-GCM
   138	    const encryptedData = aesGcmEncrypt(plainTextBuf, key, iv, aad);
   139	
   140	    // Step 7: Concatenate the encapsulated key and the encrypted data for output
   141	    const compressedSenderBuf = compressRawPublicKey(senderPubBuf);
   142	    const result = new Uint8Array(
   143	      compressedSenderBuf.length + encryptedData.length,
   144	    );
   145	    result.set(compressedSenderBuf, 0);
   146	    result.set(encryptedData, compressedSenderBuf.length);
   147	
   148	    return result;
   149	  } catch (error) {
   150	    throw new Error(`Unable to perform hpkeEncrypt: ${error}`);
   151	  }
   152	};
   153	
   154	/**
   155	 * HPKE Encrypt Function
   156	 * Encrypts data using Authenticated ,Hybrid Public Key Encryption (HPKE) standard https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/rfc9180/.
   157	 *
   158	 * @param {HpkeAuthEncryptParams} params - The encryption parameters including plain text, encapsulated key, and sender private key.
   159	 * @returns {Uint8Array} - The encrypted data.
   160	 */
   161	
   162	export const hpkeAuthEncrypt = ({
   163	  plainTextBuf,
   164	  targetKeyBuf,
   165	  senderPriv,
   166	}: HpkeAuthEncryptParams): Uint8Array => {
   167	  try {
   168	    // Authenticated HPKE Mode
   169	    const senderPrivBuf = uint8ArrayFromHexString(senderPriv);
   170	    const senderPubBuf = getPublicKey(senderPriv, false);
   171	
   172	    const aad = buildAdditionalAssociatedData(senderPubBuf, targetKeyBuf);
   173	
   174	    // Step 1: Generate Shared Secret
   175	    const ss = deriveSS(targetKeyBuf, uint8ArrayToHexString(senderPrivBuf!));
   176	
   177	    // Step 2: Generate the KEM context
   178	    const kemContext = getKemContext(
   179	      senderPubBuf,
   180	      uint8ArrayToHexString(targetKeyBuf),
   181	    );
   182	
   183	    // Step 3: Build the HKDF inputs for key derivation
   184	    let ikm = buildLabeledIkm(LABEL_EAE_PRK, ss, SUITE_ID_1);
   185	    let info = buildLabeledInfo(
   186	      LABEL_SHARED_SECRET,
   187	      kemContext,
   188	      SUITE_ID_1,
   189	      32,
   190	    );
   191	    const sharedSecret = extractAndExpand(new Uint8Array([]), ikm, info, 32);
   192	
   193	    // Step 4: Derive the AES key
   194	    ikm = buildLabeledIkm(LABEL_SECRET, new Uint8Array([]), SUITE_ID_2);
   195	    info = AES_KEY_INFO;
   196	    const key = extractAndExpand(sharedSecret, ikm, info, 32);
   197	
   198	    // Step 5: Derive the initialization vector
   199	    info = IV_INFO;
   200	    const iv = extractAndExpand(sharedSecret, ikm, info, 12);
   201	
   202	    // Step 6: Encrypt the data using AES-GCM
   203	    const encryptedData = aesGcmEncrypt(plainTextBuf, key, iv, aad);
   204	
   205	    // Step 7: Concatenate the encapsulated key and the encrypted data for output
   206	    const compressedSenderBuf = compressRawPublicKey(senderPubBuf);
   207	    const result = new Uint8Array(
   208	      compressedSenderBuf.length + encryptedData.length,
   209	    );
   210	    result.set(compressedSenderBuf, 0);
   211	    result.set(encryptedData, compressedSenderBuf.length);
   212	    return result;
   213	  } catch (error) {
   214	    throw new Error(`Unable to perform hpkeEncrypt: ${error}`);
   215	  }
   216	};
   217	
   218	/**
   219	 * Encrypt a message to a quorum key. Algorithm originally implemented in qos here: https://github.com/tkhq/qos/blob/ae01904c756107f850aea42000137ef124df3fe4/src/qos_p256/src/encrypt.rs#L123
   220	 * Returns a borsh serialized encrypted Envelope which is the nonce + ephemeralSenderPublicKey + encryptedMessage
   221	 * This function creates an ephemeral key, creates a shared secret with the recipient targetPublicKeyUncompressed
   222	 * creates additional associated data which follows the form: sender_public||sender_public_len||receiver_public||receiver_public_len
   223	 * encrypts using aes-gcm-256 with a SHA-512 HMAC over the QOS_ENCRYPTION_HMAC_MESSAGE literally: "qos_encryption_hmac_message"
   224	 * inserts and returns the necessary information in a borsh serialized envelope as described above
   225	 * This encryption function is meant to be used with this decryption function in QOS: https://github.com/tkhq/qos/blob/ae01904c756107f850aea42000137ef124df3fe4/src/qos_p256/src/encrypt.rs#L52
   226	 *
   227	 * @param {Uint8Array} targetPublicKeyUncompressed - The P256 uncompressed public key to encrypt the message to
   228	 * @param {Uint8Array} message - The message to encrypt to targetPublicKeyUncompressed
   229	 * @returns {Uint8Array} - A borsh serialized envelope containing the nonce + ephemeralSenderPublicKey + encrypted message
   230	 */
   231	export const quorumKeyEncrypt = async (
   232	  targetPublicKeyUncompressed: Uint8Array,
   233	  message: Uint8Array,
   234	): Promise<Uint8Array> => {
   235	  // generate an ephemeral keypair for this encryption operation
   236	  const ephemeralKeyPair = generateP256KeyPair();
   237	  const ephemeralSenderPublic = ephemeralKeyPair.publicKeyUncompressed;
   238	
   239	  // create a shared secret AES-GCM key with the SHA-512 HMAC
   240	  let cipher = await createQuorumKeyEncryptCipher(
   241	    uint8ArrayFromHexString(ephemeralSenderPublic),
   242	    uint8ArrayFromHexString(ephemeralKeyPair.privateKey),
   243	    targetPublicKeyUncompressed,
   244	  );
   245	
   246	  // generate a nonce
   247	  const nonce = new Uint8Array(QUORUM_ENCRYPT_NONCE_LENGTH_BYTES);
   248	  crypto.getRandomValues(nonce);
   249	
   250	  // create the additional data in the form of sender_public||sender_public_len||receiver_public||receiver_public_len taken from QOS here: https://github.com/tkhq/qos/blob/ae01904c756107f850aea42000137ef124df3fe4/src/qos_p256/src/encrypt.rs#L298
   251	  const aad = createAdditionalAssociatedData(
   252	    uint8ArrayFromHexString(ephemeralSenderPublic),
   253	    targetPublicKeyUncompressed,
   254	  );
   255	
   256	  // algorithm specifications for AES-GCM
   257	  const alg: AesGcmParams = {
   258	    name: "AES-GCM",
   259	    iv: nonce,
   260	    tagLength: 128,
   540	};
   541	
   542	/**
   543	 * Perform HKDF extract and expand operations.
   544	 */
   545	const extractAndExpand = (
   546	  sharedSecret: Uint8Array,
   547	  ikm: Uint8Array,
   548	  info: Uint8Array,
   549	  len: number,
   550	): Uint8Array => {
   551	  const prk = hkdf.extract(sha256, ikm, sharedSecret);
   552	  const resp = hkdf.expand(sha256, prk, info, len);
   553	  return new Uint8Array(resp);
   554	};
   555	
   556	/**
   557	 * Derive the Diffie-Hellman shared secret using ECDH.
   558	 */
   559	const deriveSS = (encappedKeyBuf: Uint8Array, priv: string): Uint8Array => {
   560	  const ss = p256.getSharedSecret(
   561	    uint8ArrayFromHexString(priv),
   562	    encappedKeyBuf,
   563	  );
   564	  return ss.slice(1);
   565	};
   566	
   567	/**
   568	 * Encrypt data using AES-GCM.
   569	 */
   570	const aesGcmEncrypt = (
   571	  plainTextData: Uint8Array,
   572	  key: Uint8Array,
   573	  iv: Uint8Array,
   574	  aad?: Uint8Array,
   575	): Uint8Array => {
   576	  const aes = gcm(key, iv, aad);
   577	  const data = aes.encrypt(plainTextData);
   578	  return data;
   579	};
   580	
   581	/**
   582	 * Decrypt data using AES-GCM.
   583	 */
   584	const aesGcmDecrypt = (
   585	  encryptedData: Uint8Array,
   586	  key: Uint8Array,
   587	  iv: Uint8Array,
   588	  aad?: Uint8Array,
   589	): Uint8Array => {
   590	  const aes = gcm(key, iv, aad);
   591	  const data = aes.decrypt(encryptedData);
   592	  return data;
   593	};
   594	
   595	/**
   596	 * Generate a Key Encapsulation Mechanism (KEM) context.
   597	 */
   598	const getKemContext = (
   599	  encappedKeyBuf: Uint8Array,
   600	  publicKey: string,
   601	): Uint8Array => {
   602	  const encappedKeyArray = new Uint8Array(encappedKeyBuf);
   603	  const publicKeyArray = uint8ArrayFromHexString(publicKey);
   604	
   605	  const kemContext = new Uint8Array(
   606	    encappedKeyArray.length + publicKeyArray.length,
   607	  );
   608	  kemContext.set(encappedKeyArray);
   609	  kemContext.set(publicKeyArray, encappedKeyArray.length);
   610	
   611	  return kemContext;
   612	};
   613	
   614	/**
   615	 * Convert a BigInt to a hexadecimal string of a specific length.
   616	 */
   617	const bigIntToHex = (num: bigint, length: number): string => {
   618	  const hexString = num.toString(16);
   619	  if (hexString.length > length) {
   620	    throw new Error(
   621	      `number cannot fit in a hex string of ${length} characters`,
   622	    );
   623	  }
   624	  return hexString.padStart(length, "0");
   625	};
   626	
   627	/**
   628	 * Converts an ASN.1 DER-encoded ECDSA signature to the raw format used for verification.
   629	 *
   630	 * @param {string} derSignature - The DER-encoded signature.
   631	 * @returns {Uint8Array} - The raw signature.
   632	 */
   633	export const fromDerSignature = (derSignature: string): Uint8Array => {
   634	  const derSignatureBuf = uint8ArrayFromHexString(derSignature);
   635	
   636	  // Check minimum length
   637	  if (derSignatureBuf.length < 2) {
   638	    throw new Error(
   639	      "failed to convert DER-encoded signature: insufficient length",
   640	    );
   641	  }
   642	
   643	  // Check SEQUENCE tag (0x30 at first byte)
   644	  if (derSignatureBuf[0] !== 0x30) {
   645	    throw new Error(
   646	      "failed to convert DER-encoded signature: invalid format (missing SEQUENCE tag)",
   647	    );
   648	  }
   649	
   650	  // Check second byte, start of length field
   651	  let index = 1;
   652	  const lengthByte = derSignatureBuf[index]!;
   653	  if (lengthByte <= 0x7f) {
   654	    // Short form: single byte length
   655	
   656	    // directly take the consumed value as length and check against buffer
   657	    // buffer length: initial header bytes + claimed remaining length
   658	    if (derSignatureBuf.length < 1 + 1 + lengthByte) {
   659	      throw new Error(
   660	        "failed to convert DER-encoded signature: inconsistent message length header",
   661	      );
   662	    }
   663	
   664	    // continue parsing
   665	    index += 1;
   666	  } else {
   667	    // Multi-byte DER length header
   668	    // Invalid DER values: lengthByte 0x80 and 0xff
   669	    // Valid DER values: lengthByte > 0x80, < 0xff
   670	    //
   671	    // We do not expect signature data in the Long form notation
   672	    // -> reject all such inputs
   673	    //
   674	    // More complex parsing for longer signature sequences can be implemented once needed
   675	    throw new Error(
   676	      "failed to convert DER-encoded signature: unexpectedly large or invalid signature length",
   677	    );
   678	  }
   679	
   680	  // Parse 'r' and check for integer tag (0x02)
   681	  if (derSignatureBuf[index] !== 0x02) {
   682	    throw new Error(
   683	      "failed to convert DER-encoded signature: invalid tag for r",
   684	    );
   685	  }
   686	
   687	  index++; // Move past the INTEGER tag
   688	  const rLength = derSignatureBuf[index]!;
   689	
   690	  // Allow up to 32 data bytes + 1 byte 0-padding prefix
   691	  if (rLength > 33) {
   692	    throw new Error(
   693	      "failed to convert DER-encoded signature: unexpected length for r",
   694	    );
   695	  }
   696	
   697	  index++; // Move past the length byte
   698	  const r = derSignatureBuf.slice(index, index + rLength);
   699	  index += rLength; // Move to the start of s
   700

     1	/// <reference lib="dom" />
     2	import { stringToBase64urlString } from "@turnkey/encoding";
     3	
     4	// Header name for an API key stamp
     5	const stampHeaderName = "X-Stamp";
     6	
     7	export type Runtime = "browser" | "node" | "purejs";
     8	
     9	export type TApiKeyStamperConfig = {
    10	  apiPublicKey: string;
    11	  apiPrivateKey: string;
    12	  runtimeOverride?: Runtime | undefined;
    13	};
    14	
    15	// `window.document` ensures that we're in a browser context
    16	// and `crypto.subtle` ensures that it supports the web crypto APIs
    17	// Inspired by https://github.com/flexdinesh/browser-or-node/blob/master/src/index.ts
    18	const isCryptoEnabledBrowser: boolean =
    19	  typeof window !== "undefined" &&
    20	  typeof window.document !== "undefined" &&
    21	  typeof crypto !== "undefined" &&
    22	  typeof crypto.subtle !== "undefined";
    23	
    24	// We check `process.versions.node`
    25	// Taken from https://github.com/flexdinesh/browser-or-node/blob/master/src/index.ts
    26	const isNode: boolean =
    27	  typeof process !== "undefined" &&
    28	  process.versions != null &&
    29	  process.versions.node != null;
    30	
    31	const detectRuntime = (): Runtime => {
    32	  if (isCryptoEnabledBrowser) {
    33	    return "browser";
    34	  }
    35	
    36	  if (isNode) {
    37	    return "node";
    38	  }
    39	
    40	  // If we don't have NodeJS or web crypto at our disposal, default to pure JS implementation
    41	  // This is the case for old browsers and react native environments
    42	  return "purejs";
    43	};
    44	
    45	/**
    46	 * Signature function abstracting the differences between NodeJS and web environments for signing with API keys.
    47	 */
    48	export const signWithApiKey = async (
    49	  input: {
    50	    content: string;
    51	    publicKey: string;
    52	    privateKey: string;
    53	  },
    54	  runtimeOverride?: Runtime | undefined,
    55	): Promise<string> => {
    56	  const runtime = runtimeOverride ?? detectRuntime();
    57	
    58	  switch (runtime) {
    59	    case "browser":
    60	      return (await import("./webcrypto")).signWithApiKey(input);
    61	    case "node":
    62	      return (await import("./nodecrypto")).signWithApiKey(input);
    63	    case "purejs":
    64	      return (await import("./purejs")).signWithApiKey(input);
    65	    default:
    66	      throw new Error(`Unsupported runtime: ${runtime}`);
    67	  }
    68	};
    69	
    70	/**
    71	 * Stamper to use with `@turnkey/http`'s `TurnkeyClient`
    72	 */
    73	export class ApiKeyStamper {
    74	  apiPublicKey: string;
    75	  apiPrivateKey: string;
    76	  runtimeOverride?: Runtime | undefined;
    77	
    78	  constructor(config: TApiKeyStamperConfig) {
    79	    this.apiPublicKey = config.apiPublicKey;
    80	    this.apiPrivateKey = config.apiPrivateKey;
    81	    this.runtimeOverride = config.runtimeOverride;
    82	  }
    83	
    84	  async stamp(payload: string) {
    85	    const signature = await signWithApiKey(
    86	      {
    87	        publicKey: this.apiPublicKey,
    88	        privateKey: this.apiPrivateKey,
    89	        content: payload,
    90	      },
    91	      this.runtimeOverride,
    92	    );
    93	
    94	    const stamp = {
    95	      publicKey: this.apiPublicKey,
    96	      scheme: "SIGNATURE_SCHEME_TK_API_P256",
    97	      signature,
    98	    };
    99	
   100	    return {
   101	      stampHeaderName,
   102	      stampHeaderValue: stringToBase64urlString(JSON.stringify(stamp)),
   103	    };
   104	  }
   105	}
   106	
   107	export { pointDecode } from "./tink/elliptic_curves";
     1	import { p256 } from "@noble/curves/p256";
     2	import { createHash } from "sha256-uint8array";
     3	import { uint8ArrayToHexString } from "@turnkey/encoding";
     4	
     5	export const signWithApiKey = async (input: {
     6	  content: string;
     7	  publicKey: string;
     8	  privateKey: string;
     9	}): Promise<string> => {
    10	  const publicKey = p256.getPublicKey(input.privateKey, true);
    11	
    12	  // Public key in the usual 02 or 03 + 64 hex digits
    13	  const publicKeyString = uint8ArrayToHexString(publicKey);
    14	
    15	  if (publicKeyString != input.publicKey) {
    16	    throw new Error(
    17	      `Bad API key. Expected to get public key ${input.publicKey}, got ${publicKeyString}`,
    18	    );
    19	  }
    20	
    21	  const hash = createHash().update(input.content).digest();
    22	  const signature = p256.sign(hash, input.privateKey);
    23	  return signature.toDERHex();
    24	};

     7	    ".": {
     8	      "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",

   283	 * @returns {string} - A JSON string with "encappedPublic" and "ciphertext"
   284	 */
   285	
   286	export const formatHpkeBuf = (encryptedBuf: Uint8Array): string => {
   287	  const compressedSenderBuf = encryptedBuf.slice(0, 33);
   288	  const encryptedData = encryptedBuf.slice(33);
   289	
   290	  const encappedKeyBufHex = uint8ArrayToHexString(
   291	    uncompressRawPublicKey(compressedSenderBuf),
   292	  );

   294

   297	    ciphertext: ciphertextHex,
   298	  });
   299	};
   300	
   301	/**
   302	 * HPKE Decrypt Function
   303	 * Decrypts data using Hybrid Public Key Encryption (HPKE) standard https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/rfc9180/.
   304	 *
   305	 * @param {HpkeDecryptParams} params - The decryption parameters including ciphertext, encapsulated key, and receiver private key.
   306	 * @returns {Uint8Array} - The decrypted data.
   307	 */
   308	export const hpkeDecrypt = ({
   309	  ciphertextBuf,
   310	  encappedKeyBuf,
   311	  receiverPriv,
   312	}: HpkeDecryptParams): Uint8Array => {
   313	  try {
   314	    let ikm: Uint8Array;
   315	    let info: Uint8Array;
   316	    const receiverPubBuf = getPublicKey(
   317	      uint8ArrayFromHexString(receiverPriv),
   318	      false,
   319	    );
   320	    const aad = buildAdditionalAssociatedData(encappedKeyBuf, receiverPubBuf); // Eventually we want users to be able to pass in aad as optional
   321	
   322	    // Step 1: Generate Shared Secret
   323	    const ss = deriveSS(encappedKeyBuf, receiverPriv);
   324	
   325	    // Step 2: Generate the KEM context
   326	    const kemContext = getKemContext(
   327	      encappedKeyBuf,
   328	      uint8ArrayToHexString(receiverPubBuf),
   329	    );
   330	
   331	    // Step 3: Build the HKDF inputs for key derivation
   332	    ikm = buildLabeledIkm(LABEL_EAE_PRK, ss, SUITE_ID_1);
   333	    info = buildLabeledInfo(LABEL_SHARED_SECRET, kemContext, SUITE_ID_1, 32);
   334	    const sharedSecret = extractAndExpand(new Uint8Array([]), ikm, info, 32);
   335	
   336	    // Step 4: Derive the AES key
   337	    ikm = buildLabeledIkm(LABEL_SECRET, new Uint8Array([]), SUITE_ID_2);
   338	    info = AES_KEY_INFO;
   339	    const key = extractAndExpand(sharedSecret, ikm, info, 32);
   340	
   341	    // Step 5: Derive the initialization vector
   342	    info = IV_INFO;
   343	    const iv = extractAndExpand(sharedSecret, ikm, info, 12);
   344	
   345	    // Step 6: Decrypt the data using AES-GCM
   346	    const decryptedData = aesGcmDecrypt(ciphertextBuf, key, iv, aad);
   347	    return decryptedData;
   348	  } catch (error) {
   349	    throw new Error(`Unable to perform hpkeDecrypt: ${error} `);
   350	  }
   351	};

   515	/**
   516	 * Build labeled info for HKDF operations.
   517	 *
   518	 * @param {Uint8Array} label - The label to use.
   519	 * @param {Uint8Array} info - Additional information.

   523	 */
   524	const buildLabeledInfo = (
   525	  label: Uint8Array,
   526	  info: Uint8Array,
   527	  suiteId: Uint8Array,
   528	  len: number,
   529	): Uint8Array => {
   530	  const suiteIdStartIndex = 9; // first two are reserved for length bytes (unused in this case), the next 7 are for the HPKE_VERSION, then the suiteId starts at 9
   531	  const ret = new Uint8Array(
   532	    suiteIdStartIndex + suiteId.byteLength + label.byteLength + info.byteLength,

FILE:.github/workflows/ci.yml
     1	name: CI
     2	
     3	on:
     4	  push:
     5	    branches: [main]
     6	  pull_request:
     7	    branches: [main]
     8	
     9	permissions:
    10	  contents: read
    11	
    12	jobs:
    13	  build-test:
    14	    name: Build + test (ubuntu-latest, net8.0 runner)
    15	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    16	    steps:
    17	      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
    18	
    19	      - name: Setup .NET 8
    20	        uses: actions/setup-dotnet@v5
    21	        with:
    22	          dotnet-version: 8.0.x
    23	
    24	      - name: Verify packages.lock.json is present
    25	        run: |
    26	          test -f src/packages.lock.json   || (echo "src/packages.lock.json missing"   && exit 1)
    27	          test -f tests/packages.lock.json || (echo "tests/packages.lock.json missing" && exit 1)
    28	
    29	      - name: Restore (locked-mode)
    30	        run: dotnet restore turnkey-sdk-csharp.sln --locked-mode
    31	
    32	      - name: Build (Release, both TFMs)
    33	        run: dotnet build turnkey-sdk-csharp.sln -c Release --no-restore
    34	
    35	      - name: Test (Release, with coverage)
    36	        env:
    37	          # E2E tests skip themselves if these are unset
    38	          TURNKEY_TEST_ORG_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.TURNKEY_TEST_ORG_API_KEY }}
    39	          TURNKEY_TEST_ORG_ID: ${{ secrets.TURNKEY_TEST_ORG_ID }}
    40	        run: |
    41	          dotnet test turnkey-sdk-csharp.sln -c Release --no-build \
    42	            --collect:"XPlat Code Coverage" \
    43	            --results-directory ./TestResults \
    44	            --logger "trx;LogFileName=test-results.trx"
    45	
    46	      # Coverage gate. Threshold is set to 30% combined-TFM for
    47	      # v0.1.0-alpha because the report counts both the netstandard2.1
    48	      # and net8.0 production assemblies (tests only target net8.0) AND
    49	      # the System.Text.Json source-generated TypeInfo classes (most of
    50	      # which are never executed by tests). The local single-TFM
    51	      # net8.0-only figure is ~78% line.
    52	      # TODOs to raise back:
    53	      #   1. Filter the cobertura output to net8.0 only (or unify reports).
    54	      #   2. Add targeted negative tests for
    55	      #      Crypto.DecryptCredentialBundle / EncryptPrivateKeyToBundle /
    56	      #      DecryptExportBundle error paths once Turnkey sample bundles
    57	      #      are committable.
    58	      - name: Coverage gate (>=30% combined-TFM line for v0.1.0-alpha)
    59	        uses: irongut/CodeCoverageSummary@v1.3.0
    60	        with:
    61	          filename: TestResults/**/coverage.cobertura.xml
    62	          badge: false
    63	          fail_below_min: true
    64	          thresholds: '30 50'
    65	          format: markdown
    66	          output: both
    67	
    68	      - name: Upload coverage artifact
    69	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v7
    70	        if: always()
    71	        with:
    72	          name: coverage-report
    73	          path: TestResults/**
    74	
    75	  pack:
    76	    name: dotnet pack (validation)
    77	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    78	    needs: build-test
    79	    steps:
    80	      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
    81	      - uses: actions/setup-dotnet@v5
    82	        with:
    83	          dotnet-version: 8.0.x
    84	      - name: Restore (locked-mode)
    85	        run: dotnet restore turnkey-sdk-csharp.sln --locked-mode
    86	      - name: Pack (Release)
    87	        run: |
    88	          dotnet pack src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj -c Release \
    89	            --no-restore --output ./artifacts
    90	      - name: Validate NuGet contents (README, LICENSE, NOTICE)
    91	        run: |
    92	          set -euo pipefail
    93	          NUPKG=$(ls ./artifacts/KyuzanInc.Turnkey.Sdk.*.nupkg | head -1)
    94	          echo "Validating $NUPKG"
    95	          unzip -l "$NUPKG" | tee /tmp/contents.txt
    96	          grep -q 'README.md' /tmp/contents.txt
    97	          grep -q 'LICENSE'   /tmp/contents.txt
    98	          grep -q 'NOTICE'    /tmp/contents.txt
    99	      - name: Upload .nupkg + .snupkg artifacts
   100	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v7
   101	        with:
   102	          name: nupkgs
   103	          path: ./artifacts/**
FILE:.github/workflows/consumer-smoke.yml
     1	name: Consumer install smoke
     2	
     3	# Verifies that a fresh consumer project can install
     4	# KyuzanInc.Turnkey.Sdk@<version> from nuget.pkg.github.com/KyuzanInc
     5	# and exercise the public API surface (Turnkey.Encoding +
     6	# Turnkey.Crypto + Turnkey.ApiKeyStamper).
     7	#
     8	# This is the canonical "consumer in a GitHub Actions job" pattern
     9	# documented in docs/release-process.md §5. The runner-issued
    10	# `secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN` carries `read:packages` for the same org by
    11	# default, so no PAT is needed.
    12	#
    13	# Two trigger paths:
    14	#
    15	#   1. workflow_dispatch — manual run on demand. The user supplies
    16	#      the package version and TFM through the inputs.
    17	#
    18	#   2. workflow_run — fires automatically whenever the upstream
    19	#      `Release` workflow completes successfully. The version is
    20	#      derived from the upstream tag (workflow_run.head_branch).
    21	#      `workflow_run` always executes the workflow file that exists
    22	#      on the default branch, so the new trigger is inert on feature
    23	#      branches and only activates once merged to `main`.
    24	
    25	run-name: "Consumer smoke (${{ inputs.package_version || github.event.workflow_run.head_branch || 'unknown' }})"
    26	
    27	on:
    28	  workflow_dispatch:
    29	    inputs:
    30	      package_version:
    31	        description: "Version to install (e.g. 0.1.0-alpha.0)"
    32	        required: true
    33	        default: "0.1.0-alpha.0"
    34	      tfm:
    35	        description: "Target framework moniker"
    36	        required: true
    37	        default: "net8.0"
    38	  workflow_run:
    39	    workflows: ["Release"]   # matches release.yml's `name:` field, not the filename
    40	    types: [completed]
    41	
    42	permissions:
    43	  contents: read
    44	  packages: read
    45	
    46	jobs:
    47	  consumer-install:
    48	    # Title falls back to the upstream tag short name on workflow_run
    49	    # so the job card never shows a dangling "Install + use " on
    50	    # auto-runs (where `inputs.package_version` stringifies to '').
    51	    name: "Install + use ${{ inputs.package_version || github.event.workflow_run.head_branch }}"
    52	    # Skip auto-runs that follow a failed/cancelled Release — there's
    53	    # no package to verify in that case. Manual dispatch always runs.
    54	    if: |
    55	      github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' ||
    56	      (github.event_name == 'workflow_run' && github.event.workflow_run.conclusion == 'success')
    57	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    58	    steps:
    59	      # Resolve which package version + TFM this run should install.
    60	      # `workflow_run` events do not populate the `inputs` context
    61	      # (`inputs` is only available for `workflow_dispatch` and
    62	      # `workflow_call` events per GitHub Actions context rules), so
    63	      # this step branches on `github.event_name` and pulls the
    64	      # version from the upstream tag short name for auto-runs. On
    65	      # manual runs it routes the user inputs through unchanged.
    66	      #
    67	      # Outputs:
    68	      #   - steps.resolve.outputs.package_version
    69	      #   - steps.resolve.outputs.tfm
    70	      - name: Resolve install inputs
    71	        id: resolve
    72	        env:
    73	          EVENT_NAME:   ${{ github.event_name }}
    74	          DISPATCH_VER: ${{ inputs.package_version }}
    75	          DISPATCH_TFM: ${{ inputs.tfm }}
    76	          WR_BRANCH:    ${{ github.event.workflow_run.head_branch }}
    77	          WR_EVENT:     ${{ github.event.workflow_run.event }}
    78	        run: |
    79	          set -euo pipefail
    80	          if [[ "$EVENT_NAME" == "workflow_dispatch" ]]; then
    81	            VER="$DISPATCH_VER"
    82	            TFM="$DISPATCH_TFM"
    83	          else
    84	            # workflow_run from `release.yml`. The upstream Release
    85	            # workflow only triggers on `release: published`, so
    86	            # `workflow_run.event` MUST be 'release' and
    87	            # `workflow_run.head_branch` is the tag ref short name
    88	            # (e.g. 'v0.1.0-alpha.0'). Refuse anything else.
    89	            if [[ "$WR_EVENT" != "release" ]]; then
    90	              echo "::error::Unexpected upstream workflow_run.event '$WR_EVENT' (expected 'release'). Refusing to run."
    91	              exit 1
    92	            fi
    93	            # Strict SemVer 2.0.0 with leading 'v' — kept BYTE-FOR-BYTE
    94	            # in sync with the regex in
    95	            # `.github/workflows/release.yml` step "Parse + validate
    96	            # strict SemVer 2.0.0 tag". Any drift here is a bug.
    97	            re='^v(0|[1-9][0-9]*)\.(0|[1-9][0-9]*)\.(0|[1-9][0-9]*)(-((0|[1-9][0-9]*|[0-9]*[A-Za-z-][0-9A-Za-z-]*)(\.(0|[1-9][0-9]*|[0-9]*[A-Za-z-][0-9A-Za-z-]*))*))?$'
    98	            if [[ ! "$WR_BRANCH" =~ $re ]]; then
    99	              echo "::error::workflow_run.head_branch '$WR_BRANCH' is not a strict SemVer 2.0.0 tag; refusing to install."
   100	              exit 1
   101	            fi
   102	            VER="${WR_BRANCH#v}"
   103	            TFM="net8.0"  # default for auto-runs; matrix-able in a later plan
   104	          fi
   105	          echo "package_version=$VER" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
   106	          echo "tfm=$TFM"             >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
   107	          echo "Resolved: package_version=$VER, tfm=$TFM (source=$EVENT_NAME)"
   108	
   109	      # actions/checkout v6.0.2 — see .github/pin-actions.txt
   110	      # Checkout is only needed so setup-dotnet can find a workspace.
   111	      - name: Checkout (workspace anchor)
   112	        uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd
   113	        with:
   114	          persist-credentials: false
   115	
   116	      # actions/setup-dotnet v5.2.0 — see .github/pin-actions.txt
   117	      - name: Setup .NET 8
   118	        uses: actions/setup-dotnet@c2fa09f4bde5ebb9d1777cf28262a3eb3db3ced7
   119	        with:
   120	          dotnet-version: 8.0.x
   121	
   122	      - name: Create fresh consumer project
   123	        env:
   124	          TFM: ${{ steps.resolve.outputs.tfm }}
   125	        run: |
   126	          set -euo pipefail
   127	          mkdir -p /tmp/consumer && cd /tmp/consumer
   128	          dotnet new console --framework "$TFM" --name TurnkeySdkConsumer --force
   129	
   130	      - name: Configure GitHub Packages source via dotnet nuget add source
   131	        env:
   132	          GH_ACTOR: ${{ github.actor }}
   133	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
   134	        run: |
   135	          set -euo pipefail
   136	          cd /tmp/consumer/TurnkeySdkConsumer
   137	          # Write the source with credentials into a LOCAL NuGet.config.
   138	          # Using `dotnet nuget add source` lets the CLI handle escaping
   139	          # of the URL/username/token. The resulting NuGet.config
   140	          # contains the token in clear text, which is acceptable here
   141	          # because (a) the file lives inside an ephemeral runner only,
   142	          # and (b) `secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN` is automatically revoked at
   143	          # job end.
   144	          dotnet nuget add source \
   145	            "https://nuget.pkg.github.com/KyuzanInc/index.json" \
   146	            --name kyuzan-github \
   147	            --username "$GH_ACTOR" \
   148	            --password "$GH_TOKEN" \
   149	            --store-password-in-clear-text
   150	
   151	      - name: Install KyuzanInc.Turnkey.Sdk
   152	        env:
   153	          VERSION: ${{ steps.resolve.outputs.package_version }}
   154	        run: |
   155	          set -euo pipefail
   156	          cd /tmp/consumer/TurnkeySdkConsumer
   157	          dotnet add package KyuzanInc.Turnkey.Sdk --version "$VERSION"
   158	
   159	      - name: Write sample Program.cs (exercises public API)
   160	        run: |
   161	          set -euo pipefail
   162	          cat > /tmp/consumer/TurnkeySdkConsumer/Program.cs <<'PROGRAM'
   163	          using System;
   164	          using System.Linq;
   165	          using Turnkey;
   166	
   167	          internal static class Program
   168	          {
   169	              private static int Main()
   170	              {
   171	                  Console.WriteLine("=== KyuzanInc.Turnkey.Sdk consumer smoke ===");
   172	
   173	                  // 1. Turnkey.Encoding — hex round-trip
   174	                  byte[] sample = new byte[] { 0xDE, 0xAD, 0xBE, 0xEF, 0x00, 0x11, 0x22, 0x33 };
   175	                  string hex = Encoding.Uint8ArrayToHexString(sample);
   176	                  byte[] back = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(hex);
   177	                  bool encodingOk = back.SequenceEqual(sample) && hex == "deadbeef00112233";
   178	                  Console.WriteLine($"[1/3] Encoding.Uint8ArrayToHexString: \"{hex}\" (round-trip ok: {encodingOk})");
   179	                  if (!encodingOk) return 1;
   180	
   181	                  // 2. Turnkey.Crypto — generate a P-256 keypair
   182	                  var keyPair = Crypto.GenerateP256KeyPair();
   183	                  bool privOk = !string.IsNullOrEmpty(keyPair.PrivateKey) && keyPair.PrivateKey.Length == 64;
   184	                  bool pubOk  = !string.IsNullOrEmpty(keyPair.PublicKey)  && keyPair.PublicKey.Length  == 66;
   185	                  Console.WriteLine($"[2/3] Crypto.GenerateP256KeyPair: priv.len={keyPair.PrivateKey.Length}, pub.len={keyPair.PublicKey.Length} (ok: {privOk && pubOk})");
   186	                  if (!privOk || !pubOk) return 2;
   187	
   188	                  // 3. Turnkey.ApiKeyStamper — sign a sample payload
   189	                  var stamper = new ApiKeyStamper(keyPair.PublicKey, keyPair.PrivateKey);
   190	                  var stamp = stamper.Stamp("{\"sample\":\"payload\"}");
   191	                  bool stampOk = !string.IsNullOrEmpty(stamp.StampHeaderValue)
   192	                                 && stamp.StampHeaderName == "X-Stamp";
   193	                  Console.WriteLine($"[3/3] ApiKeyStamper.Stamp: header={stamp.StampHeaderName}, value.len={stamp.StampHeaderValue.Length} (ok: {stampOk})");
   194	                  if (!stampOk) return 3;
   195	
   196	                  Console.WriteLine();
   197	                  Console.WriteLine("Consumer install + use of KyuzanInc.Turnkey.Sdk verified end-to-end.");
   198	                  return 0;
   199	              }
   200	          }
   201	          PROGRAM
   202	
   203	      - name: Build + run consumer sample
   204	        run: |
   205	          set -euo pipefail
   206	          cd /tmp/consumer/TurnkeySdkConsumer
   207	          dotnet build -c Release
   208	          dotnet run -c Release --no-build
FILE:.github/workflows/pr-title-lint.yml
     1	name: PR title lint
     2	
     3	on:
     4	  pull_request:
     5	    types: [opened, reopened, synchronize, edited]
     6	
     7	permissions:
     8	  contents: read
     9	  pull-requests: read
    10	
    11	jobs:
    12	  lint:
    13	    # Job display name must stay in sync with the required-check name
    14	    # documented in docs/release-process.md: the resulting GitHub UI
    15	    # status is "PR title lint / Verify conventional commit title".
    16	    name: Verify conventional commit title
    17	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    18	    env:
    19	      PR_TITLE: ${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}
    20	    steps:
    21	      - name: Verify conventional commit title
    22	        run: |
    23	          set -euo pipefail
    24	          # Structural regex: <type>(<scope>)?!?: <subject>
    25	          # types: feat, fix, deps, docs, ci, chore, refactor, test
    26	          # scope (when present): non-empty
    27	          # subject: at least one char (further checked below for non-whitespace)
    28	          re='^(feat|fix|deps|docs|ci|chore|refactor|test)(\([^)]+\))?!?: (.+)$'
    29	
    30	          # Apply GitHub's workflow-command escape (per
    31	          # https://docs.github.com/en/actions/reference/workflows-and-actions/workflow-commands#example-evaluating-data-from-untrusted-sources)
    32	          # before inserting untrusted PR-title text into an ::error::
    33	          # annotation. Order matters: encode `%` first so other
    34	          # escape sequences are not double-encoded.
    35	          escape() {
    36	            local s="$1"
    37	            s="${s//%/%25}"
    38	            s="${s//$'\r'/%0D}"
    39	            s="${s//$'\n'/%0A}"
    40	            printf '%s' "$s"
    41	          }
    42	          sanitized_raw=$(printf '%s' "$PR_TITLE" | tr -cd '[:print:]\n\r' | head -c 200)
    43	          sanitized=$(escape "$sanitized_raw")
    44	
    45	          if [[ ! "$PR_TITLE" =~ $re ]]; then
    46	            echo "::error::PR title is not conventional (expect: <type>(scope)?!?: <subject>; types: feat|fix|deps|docs|ci|chore|refactor|test). See docs/release-process.md. Got: ${sanitized}"
    47	            exit 1
    48	          fi
    49	          # Subject must contain at least one non-whitespace character.
    50	          subject="${BASH_REMATCH[3]}"
    51	          if ! printf '%s' "$subject" | grep -Eq '[^[:space:]]'; then
    52	            echo "::error::PR title subject is whitespace-only. Got: ${sanitized}"
    53	            exit 1
    54	          fi
FILE:.github/workflows/release-drafter-autolabel.yml
     1	name: Release drafter — autolabel PR
     2	
     3	on:
     4	  pull_request:
     5	    # `edited` is included so title-edit relabeling triggers. The
     6	    # "Strip stale conventional-commit labels" step below runs only on
     7	    # `edited` events and removes stale conventional-commit labels
     8	    # before the autolabeler re-applies based on the current title.
     9	    # `labeled` / `unlabeled` deliberately excluded so manual maintainer
    10	    # label adjustments are not auto-reverted.
    11	    types: [opened, reopened, synchronize, edited]
    12	
    13	permissions:
    14	  contents: read
    15	  pull-requests: write
    16	
    17	jobs:
    18	  autolabel:
    19	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    20	    env:
    21	      GH_TOKEN:  ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
    22	      GH_REPO:   ${{ github.repository }}
    23	      PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
    24	    steps:
    25	      # release-drafter's autolabeler only ADDs labels; it cannot remove.
    26	      # If the title changes from "fix:" to "feat:", the stale "fix"
    27	      # label would linger and pollute the version-resolver. Strip the
    28	      # conventional-commit set first ONLY on `edited` so unmodified
    29	      # PR events keep their existing labels intact.
    30	      #
    31	      # We use `gh pr edit --remove-label` (NOT `gh label remove`, which
    32	      # would delete the label from the repository entirely). The script
    33	      # first queries which conventional-commit labels are actually
    34	      # attached to this PR, then only calls --remove-label for those.
    35	      # Any `gh` failure aborts.
    36	      - name: Strip stale conventional-commit labels (edited only)
    37	        if: github.event.action == 'edited'
    38	        run: |
    39	          set -euo pipefail
    40	          conventional='breaking feature fix deps docs ci chore'
    41	
    42	          # List labels actually attached to this PR.
    43	          attached=$(gh pr view "$PR_NUMBER" --json labels --jq '.labels[].name')
    44	
    45	          # Intersect conventional ∩ attached; remove only those.
    46	          for label in $conventional; do
    47	            if grep -Fxq "$label" <<< "$attached"; then
    48	              gh pr edit "$PR_NUMBER" --remove-label "$label"
    49	            fi
    50	          done
    51	
    52	      # release-drafter v7.3.0 autolabeler subpath
    53	      - uses: release-drafter/release-drafter/autolabeler@da8b9b696abad2ad997580e49f4c1c2e03cc2bd2
    54	        with:
    55	          config-name: release-drafter.yml
    56	        env:
    57	          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
FILE:.github/workflows/release-drafter-draft.yml
     1	name: Release drafter — update draft on main
     2	
     3	on:
     4	  push:
     5	    branches: [main]
     6	  workflow_dispatch:
     7	
     8	permissions:
     9	  contents: write
    10	  pull-requests: read
    11	
    12	jobs:
    13	  draft:
    14	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    15	    steps:
    16	      # release-drafter v7.3.0 — see .github/pin-actions.txt
    17	      - uses: release-drafter/release-drafter@da8b9b696abad2ad997580e49f4c1c2e03cc2bd2
    18	        with:
    19	          config-name: release-drafter.yml
    20	        env:
    21	          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
FILE:.github/workflows/release.yml
     1	name: Release
     2	
     3	on:
     4	  release:
     5	    types: [published]
     6	
     7	# Allow re-running the same tag if the prior run failed mid-flight,
     8	# but never two different runs in parallel for the same tag.
     9	concurrency:
    10	  group: release-${{ github.event.release.tag_name }}
    11	  cancel-in-progress: false
    12	
    13	permissions:
    14	  contents: write       # upload Release assets
    15	  packages: write       # publish to nuget.pkg.github.com
    16	  id-token: write       # attest-build-provenance OIDC
    17	  attestations: write   # attest-build-provenance writes attestations
    18	
    19	jobs:
    20	  publish:
    21	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    22	    env:
    23	      RELEASE_TAG:      ${{ github.event.release.tag_name }}
    24	      GITHUB_ACTOR_ENV: ${{ github.actor }}
    25	
    26	    steps:
    27	      # actions/checkout v6.0.2 — see .github/pin-actions.txt
    28	      - name: Checkout tag
    29	        uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd
    30	        with:
    31	          ref: ${{ github.event.release.tag_name }}
    32	          fetch-depth: 0
    33	          # Drop checkout token from .git/config after checkout — later
    34	          # git/gh calls pass `GH_TOKEN` explicitly and never authenticate
    35	          # via the persisted checkout credential, so persisting it
    36	          # extends the token's blast radius without benefit.
    37	          persist-credentials: false
    38	
    39	      - name: Parse + validate strict SemVer 2.0.0 tag
    40	        id: ver
    41	        env:
    42	          TAG: ${{ env.RELEASE_TAG }}
    43	        run: |
    44	          set -euo pipefail
    45	          # Strict SemVer 2.0.0 (no build metadata): vMAJOR.MINOR.PATCH[-PRERELEASE]
    46	          # PRERELEASE = identifier (. identifier)*
    47	          # identifier = numeric (no leading zero) | alphanumeric
    48	          re='^v(0|[1-9][0-9]*)\.(0|[1-9][0-9]*)\.(0|[1-9][0-9]*)(-((0|[1-9][0-9]*|[0-9]*[A-Za-z-][0-9A-Za-z-]*)(\.(0|[1-9][0-9]*|[0-9]*[A-Za-z-][0-9A-Za-z-]*))*))?$'
    49	          if [[ ! "$TAG" =~ $re ]]; then
    50	            echo "::error::Tag '$TAG' is not strict SemVer 2.0.0 (vMAJOR.MINOR.PATCH[-PRERELEASE]; no build metadata)."
    51	            exit 1
    52	          fi
    53	          echo "version=${TAG#v}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
    54	          echo "tag=$TAG"          >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
    55	
    56	      - name: Resolve tag commit SHA
    57	        id: tagsha
    58	        env:
    59	          TAG: ${{ env.RELEASE_TAG }}
    60	        run: |
    61	          set -euo pipefail
    62	          echo "sha=$(git rev-parse "${TAG}^{commit}")" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
    63	
    64	      - name: Verify tag commit is contained in main
    65	        env:
    66	          GH_TOKEN:   ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
    67	          OWNER_REPO: ${{ github.repository }}
    68	          TAG_SHA:    ${{ steps.tagsha.outputs.sha }}
    69	        run: |
    70	          set -euo pipefail
    71	          # Use the REST compare API instead of `git fetch origin main`.
    72	          # Reason: `actions/checkout` ran with `persist-credentials:
    73	          # false` (security), so the runner's `.git/config` has no
    74	          # credential to authenticate a `git fetch` against this
    75	          # private repo. `gh api` reads `GH_TOKEN` from the env and
    76	          # works against private repos without persisting a git
    77	          # credential.
    78	          #
    79	          # Semantics: GET /repos/{repo}/compare/{base}...{head} returns
    80	          # `status` ∈ {ahead, behind, identical, diverged}, where
    81	          # `base = main` and `head = TAG_SHA`. The tag is reachable
    82	          # from main iff main contains the tag commit, i.e. main has
    83	          # commits the tag does not (status: `behind`) or main and
    84	          # tag point to the same commit (status: `identical`). Any
    85	          # other status (`ahead`, `diverged`) means the tag carries
    86	          # commits not on main and must be rejected.
    87	          STATUS=$(gh api "repos/${OWNER_REPO}/compare/main...${TAG_SHA}" --jq .status)
    88	          case "$STATUS" in
    89	            behind|identical)
    90	              echo "tag ${TAG_SHA} reachable from main (compare status: ${STATUS})"
    91	              ;;
    92	            *)
    93	              echo "::error::Tag commit ${TAG_SHA} is not reachable from main (compare status: ${STATUS}). Releases must be cut from a commit on main."
    94	              exit 1
    95	              ;;
    96	          esac
    97	
    98	      # actions/setup-dotnet v5.2.0 — see .github/pin-actions.txt
    99	      - name: Setup .NET 8
   100	        uses: actions/setup-dotnet@c2fa09f4bde5ebb9d1777cf28262a3eb3db3ced7
   101	        with:
   102	          dotnet-version: 8.0.x
   103	
   104	      - name: Restore (locked-mode)
   105	        run: dotnet restore turnkey-sdk-csharp.sln --locked-mode
   106	
   107	      - name: Build
   108	        env:
   109	          VERSION: ${{ steps.ver.outputs.version }}
   110	          TAG_SHA: ${{ steps.tagsha.outputs.sha }}
   111	        run: |
   112	          dotnet build turnkey-sdk-csharp.sln -c Release --no-restore \
   113	            /p:Version="$VERSION" \
   114	            /p:PackageVersion="$VERSION" \
   115	            /p:RepositoryCommit="$TAG_SHA" \
   116	            /p:IncludeSymbols=true \
   117	            /p:SymbolPackageFormat=snupkg
   118	
   119	      - name: Test
   120	        env:
   121	          TURNKEY_TEST_ORG_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.TURNKEY_TEST_ORG_API_KEY }}
   122	          TURNKEY_TEST_ORG_ID:      ${{ secrets.TURNKEY_TEST_ORG_ID }}
   123	        run: dotnet test turnkey-sdk-csharp.sln -c Release --no-build
   124	
   125	      - name: Pack
   126	        env:
   127	          VERSION: ${{ steps.ver.outputs.version }}
   128	          TAG_SHA: ${{ steps.tagsha.outputs.sha }}
   129	        run: |
   130	          dotnet pack src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj -c Release --no-build \
   131	            --output ./artifacts \
   132	            /p:Version="$VERSION" \
   133	            /p:PackageVersion="$VERSION" \
   134	            /p:RepositoryCommit="$TAG_SHA" \
   135	            /p:IncludeSymbols=true \
   136	            /p:SymbolPackageFormat=snupkg
   137	
   138	      - name: Validate local .nupkg + .snupkg contents (strict)
   139	        id: pack_artifacts
   140	        env:
   141	          VERSION: ${{ steps.ver.outputs.version }}
   142	        run: |
   143	          set -euo pipefail
   144	          NUPKG="./artifacts/KyuzanInc.Turnkey.Sdk.${VERSION}.nupkg"
   145	          SNUPKG="./artifacts/KyuzanInc.Turnkey.Sdk.${VERSION}.snupkg"
   146	          test -f "$NUPKG"  || { echo "::error::nupkg missing";  exit 1; }
   147	          test -f "$SNUPKG" || { echo "::error::snupkg missing"; exit 1; }
   148	
   149	          # .nupkg: strict path enumeration + full-line fixed-string match.
   150	          nupkg_paths=$(unzip -Z1 "$NUPKG")
   151	          echo "$nupkg_paths"
   152	          for f in README.md LICENSE NOTICE \
   153	                   lib/netstandard2.1/KyuzanInc.Turnkey.Sdk.dll \
   154	                   lib/net8.0/KyuzanInc.Turnkey.Sdk.dll; do
   155	            echo "$nupkg_paths" | grep -Fxq "$f" \
   156	              || { echo "::error::nupkg is missing exact path '$f'"; exit 1; }
   157	          done
   158	
   159	          # .snupkg: must contain .pdb for both TFMs.
   160	          snupkg_paths=$(unzip -Z1 "$SNUPKG")
   161	          echo "$snupkg_paths"
   162	          for f in lib/netstandard2.1/KyuzanInc.Turnkey.Sdk.pdb \
   163	                   lib/net8.0/KyuzanInc.Turnkey.Sdk.pdb; do
   164	            echo "$snupkg_paths" | grep -Fxq "$f" \
   165	              || { echo "::error::snupkg is missing exact path '$f'"; exit 1; }
   166	          done
   167	
   168	          NUPKG_SHA256=$(sha256sum  "$NUPKG"  | awk '{print $1}')
   169	          SNUPKG_SHA256=$(sha256sum "$SNUPKG" | awk '{print $1}')
   170	          # Use step outputs (not $GITHUB_ENV) for explicit, unambiguous
   171	          # cross-step data flow. Consumers reference these as
   172	          # steps.pack_artifacts.outputs.<key> wrapped in workflow
   173	          # expression delimiters at the call site (delimiters omitted
   174	          # in this comment so the YAML parser does not interpolate it).
   175	          {
   176	            echo "nupkg_path=$NUPKG"
   177	            echo "snupkg_path=$SNUPKG"
   178	            echo "nupkg_sha256=$NUPKG_SHA256"
   179	            echo "snupkg_sha256=$SNUPKG_SHA256"
   180	          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
   181	
   182	      - name: Write release-checksums.txt
   183	        id: checksums
   184	        env:
   185	          VERSION:       ${{ steps.ver.outputs.version }}
   186	          TAG_SHA:       ${{ steps.tagsha.outputs.sha }}
   187	          NUPKG_SHA256:  ${{ steps.pack_artifacts.outputs.nupkg_sha256 }}
   188	          SNUPKG_SHA256: ${{ steps.pack_artifacts.outputs.snupkg_sha256 }}
   189	        run: |
   190	          set -euo pipefail
   191	          CHECKSUMS="./artifacts/release-checksums.txt"
   192	          {
   193	            echo "# release-checksums for KyuzanInc.Turnkey.Sdk ${VERSION}"
   194	            echo "version          = ${VERSION}"
   195	            echo "source_commit    = ${TAG_SHA}"
   196	            echo "nupkg_sha256     = ${NUPKG_SHA256}"
   197	            echo "snupkg_sha256    = ${SNUPKG_SHA256}"
   198	          } > "$CHECKSUMS"
   199	          echo "checksums_path=$CHECKSUMS" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
   200	
   201	      - name: Preflight existing Release assets (unconditional)
   202	        id: preflight
   203	        env:
   204	          GH_TOKEN:        ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
   205	          TAG:             ${{ env.RELEASE_TAG }}
   206	          VERSION:         ${{ steps.ver.outputs.version }}
   207	          NUPKG_SHA256:    ${{ steps.pack_artifacts.outputs.nupkg_sha256 }}
   208	          SNUPKG_SHA256:   ${{ steps.pack_artifacts.outputs.snupkg_sha256 }}
   209	          NUPKG_PATH:      ${{ steps.pack_artifacts.outputs.nupkg_path }}
   210	          SNUPKG_PATH:     ${{ steps.pack_artifacts.outputs.snupkg_path }}
   211	          CHECKSUMS_PATH:  ${{ steps.checksums.outputs.checksums_path }}
   212	          NUPKG_NAME:      KyuzanInc.Turnkey.Sdk.${{ steps.ver.outputs.version }}.nupkg
   213	          SNUPKG_NAME:     KyuzanInc.Turnkey.Sdk.${{ steps.ver.outputs.version }}.snupkg
   214	        run: |
   215	          set -euo pipefail
   216	          mkdir -p ./_release_assets
   217	          HAS_PRIOR=false
   218	
   219	          # Enumerate assets that actually exist on the Release. We do NOT
   220	          # treat a failed `gh release download` as "asset missing" —
FILE:.github/workflows/repo-privacy-check.yml
     1	name: Repo privacy check
     2	
     3	on:
     4	  push:
     5	    branches: [main]
     6	  schedule:
     7	    - cron: '0 9 * * 1'   # Mondays 09:00 UTC
     8	
     9	permissions:
    10	  contents: read
    11	
    12	jobs:
    13	  privacy:
    14	    name: Assert repo is PRIVATE
    15	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    16	    steps:
    17	      - name: Verify visibility
    18	        env:
    19	          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
    20	        run: |
    21	          set -euo pipefail
    22	          VIS=$(gh repo view "${{ github.repository }}" --json visibility -q .visibility)
    23	          echo "Visibility: $VIS"
    24	          if [[ "$VIS" != "PRIVATE" ]]; then
    25	            echo "::error::Repository visibility must be PRIVATE, got: $VIS"
    26	            exit 1
    27	          fi

exec

    37	//     Tonelli-Shanks, and bundle parsing logic are direct line-by-line
    38	//     ports of the upstream TypeScript.

    41	// 2.8.8 vs 2.8.9 note:

    43	//   the published dist/ is the inlining of QOS_ENCRYPTION_HMAC_MESSAGE
    44	//   (2.8.9 hard-codes the bytes; 2.8.8 uses new TextEncoder().encode("...")).
    45	//   They produce identical wire bytes. This port targets 2.8.8 (peak's pin)
    46	//   but is logically equivalent to 2.8.9 as well.
    47

   384	
   385	            var publicKeyCompressed = GetPublicKey(privateKey, isCompressed: true);
   386	            var publicKeyUncompressed = GetPublicKey(privateKey, isCompressed: false);
   387	
   388	            return new KeyPair
   389	            {
   390	                PrivateKey = Encoding.Uint8ArrayToHexString(privateKey),

   393	            };

   395	
   396	        /// <summary>
   397	        /// HPKE-Base mode decrypt (P-256 / HKDF-SHA256 / AES-128-GCM).
   398	        /// Equivalent to upstream <c>crypto.ts hpkeDecrypt</c>.
   399	        /// </summary>
   400	        /// <exception cref="InvalidOperationException">
   401	        /// Wraps any underlying decryption failure with the upstream
   402	        /// message <c>"Unable to perform hpkeDecrypt: ..."</c>.
   403	        /// </exception>
   404	        public static byte[] HpkeDecrypt(HpkeDecryptParams parameters)
   405	        {
   406	            try
   407	            {
   408	                if (parameters == null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(parameters));
   409	                var ciphertextBuf = parameters.CiphertextBuf
   410	                                    ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(parameters.CiphertextBuf));
   411	                var encappedKeyBuf = parameters.EncappedKeyBuf
   412	                                     ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(parameters.EncappedKeyBuf));
   413	                var receiverPriv = parameters.ReceiverPriv
   414	                                   ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(parameters.ReceiverPriv));
   415	
   416	                var receiverPrivBytes = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(receiverPriv);
   417	                var receiverPubBuf = GetPublicKey(receiverPrivBytes, false);
   418	
   419	                var aad = BuildAdditionalAssociatedData(encappedKeyBuf, receiverPubBuf);
   420	                var ss = DeriveSS(encappedKeyBuf, receiverPriv);
   421	                var kemContext = GetKemContext(encappedKeyBuf, Encoding.Uint8ArrayToHexString(receiverPubBuf));
   422	
   423	                var ikm = BuildLabeledIkm(Constants.LABEL_EAE_PRK, ss, Constants.SUITE_ID_1);
   424	                var info = BuildLabeledInfo(Constants.LABEL_SHARED_SECRET, kemContext, Constants.SUITE_ID_1, 32);
   425	                var sharedSecret = ExtractAndExpand(Array.Empty<byte>(), ikm, info, 32);
   426	
   427	                ikm = BuildLabeledIkm(Constants.LABEL_SECRET, Array.Empty<byte>(), Constants.SUITE_ID_2);
   428	                info = Constants.AES_KEY_INFO;
   429	                var key = ExtractAndExpand(sharedSecret, ikm, info, 32);
   430	
   431	                info = Constants.IV_INFO;
   432	                var iv = ExtractAndExpand(sharedSecret, ikm, info, 12);
   433	
   434	                return AesGcmDecrypt(ciphertextBuf, key, iv, aad);
   435	            }
   436	            catch (Exception error)
   437	            {
   438	                throw new InvalidOperationException(
   439	                    "Unable to perform hpkeDecrypt: " + error.Message + " ", error);
   440	            }
   441	        }
   442	
   443	        /// <summary>
   444	        /// HPKE-Base mode encrypt (P-256 / HKDF-SHA256 / AES-128-GCM).
   445	        /// Equivalent to upstream <c>crypto.ts hpkeEncrypt</c>.
   446	        /// </summary>
   447	        /// <returns>
   448	        /// The byte concatenation
   449	        /// <c>compressed(senderPub) || ciphertext</c>, ready to feed to
   450	        /// <see cref="FormatHpkeBuf"/> or <see cref="DecryptCredentialBundle"/>.
   451	        /// </returns>
   452	        /// <exception cref="InvalidOperationException">
   453	        /// Wraps any underlying failure with the upstream message
   454	        /// <c>"Unable to perform hpkeEncrypt: ..."</c>.
   455	        /// </exception>
   456	        public static byte[] HpkeEncrypt(HpkeEncryptParams parameters)
   457	        {
   458	            try
   459	            {
   460	                if (parameters == null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(parameters));
   461	                var plainTextBuf = parameters.PlainTextBuf
   462	                                   ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(parameters.PlainTextBuf));
   463	                var targetKeyBuf = parameters.TargetKeyBuf
   464	                                   ?? throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(parameters.TargetKeyBuf));
   465	
   466	                var ephemeralKeyPair = GenerateP256KeyPair();
   467	                var senderPrivBuf = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(ephemeralKeyPair.PrivateKey);
   468	                var senderPubBuf = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(ephemeralKeyPair.PublicKeyUncompressed);
   469	
   470	                var aad = BuildAdditionalAssociatedData(senderPubBuf, targetKeyBuf);
   820	                if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(bundleOrgId)
   821	                    || !string.Equals(bundleOrgId, parameters.OrganizationId, StringComparison.Ordinal))
   822	                {
   823	                    throw new InvalidOperationException(
   824	                        "organization id does not match expected value. Expected: " + parameters.OrganizationId
   825	                        + ". Found: " + bundleOrgId + ".");
   826	                }
   827	
   828	                string? encappedPublic = GetStringOrNull(signedData, "encappedPublic");
   829	                if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(encappedPublic))
   830	                {
   831	                    throw new InvalidOperationException("missing \"encappedPublic\" in bundle signed data");
   832	                }
   833	                string? ciphertextStr = GetStringOrNull(signedData, "ciphertext");
   834	
   835	                var encappedKeyBuf = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(encappedPublic!);
   836	                var ciphertextBuf = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(ciphertextStr!);
   837	
   838	                var decryptedData = HpkeDecrypt(new HpkeDecryptParams
   839	                {
   840	                    CiphertextBuf = ciphertextBuf,
   841	                    EncappedKeyBuf = encappedKeyBuf,
   842	                    ReceiverPriv = parameters.EmbeddedKey,
   843	                });
   844	
   845	                if (string.Equals(parameters.KeyFormat, "SOLANA", StringComparison.Ordinal)
   846	                    && !parameters.ReturnMnemonic)
   847	                {
   848	                    if (decryptedData.Length != 32)
   849	                    {
   850	                        throw new InvalidOperationException(
   851	                            "invalid private key length. Expected 32 bytes. Got " + decryptedData.Length + ".");
   852	                    }
   853	                    // Derive Ed25519 public key from the 32-byte seed via BouncyCastle.
   854	                    var ed25519PrivKey = new Ed25519PrivateKeyParameters(decryptedData, 0);
   855	                    var publicKeyBytes = ed25519PrivKey.GeneratePublicKey().GetEncoded();
   856	                    if (publicKeyBytes.Length != 32)
   857	                    {
   858	                        throw new InvalidOperationException(
   859	                            "invalid public key length. Expected 32 bytes. Got " + publicKeyBytes.Length + ".");
   860	                    }
   861	                    var concatenated = new byte[64];
   862	                    Array.Copy(decryptedData, 0, concatenated, 0, 32);
   863	                    Array.Copy(publicKeyBytes, 0, concatenated, 32, 32);
   864	                    return Encoding.Base58Encode(concatenated);
   865	                }
   866	
   867	                var decryptedHex = Encoding.Uint8ArrayToHexString(decryptedData);
   868	                return parameters.ReturnMnemonic ? Encoding.HexToAscii(decryptedHex) : decryptedHex;
   869	            }
   870	            catch (Exception error)
   871	            {
   872	                throw new InvalidOperationException(
   873	                    "Error decrypting bundle: " + error.Message, error);
   874	            }
   875	        }

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# Turnkey upstream source pins
     2	
     3	The C# port targets the **peak-pinned** Turnkey npm versions, NOT the Unity
     4	port's newer versions. The npm tarball contents (extracted under
     5	[`upstream-snapshots/`](./upstream-snapshots/)) are the authoritative source.
     6	GitHub SHAs are secondary metadata.
     7	
     8	## Pin table
     9	
    10	| src C# file                | Upstream snapshot path                  | npm package                | Version | Tarball sha256 |
    11	|---|---|---|---|---|
    12	| src/Encoding.cs            | turnkey-encoding-0.6.0                  | @turnkey/encoding          | 0.6.0   | `2cf9e6ee1f47ac7e3cc3e644cdb0e3e112c906a6ea1af737777f4658b73fb7bc` |
    13	| src/CryptoConstants.cs     | turnkey-crypto-2.8.8                    | @turnkey/crypto            | 2.8.8   | `75115706fccc29c664f9f918d9a0c2c1798eb03f261cb5b0c186c75663cf79d3` |
    14	| src/Crypto.cs              | turnkey-crypto-2.8.8                    | @turnkey/crypto            | 2.8.8   | `75115706fccc29c664f9f918d9a0c2c1798eb03f261cb5b0c186c75663cf79d3` |
    15	| src/ApiKeyStamper.cs       | turnkey-api-key-stamper-0.5.0           | @turnkey/api-key-stamper   | 0.5.0   | `962a2d22c7c40240f05be98533769b37ab7dad7dbb5abec762c41007233d02bd` |
    16	| src/Http.cs                | turnkey-http-3.16.0                     | @turnkey/http              | 3.16.0  | `d849f2156633f63062c52067785df1ce33eac0659044df932862c5d6de9dbdaf` |
    17	| src/TurnkeyJsonContext.cs  | turnkey-crypto-2.8.8                    | (no single upstream; covers DTOs from crypto + api-key-stamper + http) | n/a | n/a |
    18	
    19	Each upstream snapshot directory contains:
    20	
    21	- `package/` — verbatim npm tarball extract (the authoritative wire-format
    22	  source: `dist/*.js` + `dist/*.d.ts` + `package.json` + LICENSE / README).
    23	- `ts-source/` — the original TypeScript source from `github.com/tkhq/sdk` at
    24	  the matching git tag SHA (committed for human readability; the npm tarball
    25	  remains the wire-format source of truth if the two ever disagree).
    26	
    27	## Why these versions
    28	
    29	These are exactly what the peak monorepo pulls. See
    30	[`peak-lockfile-evidence.md`](./peak-lockfile-evidence.md) for the
    31	`pnpm-lock.yaml` lines.
    32	
    33	## GitHub commit SHAs (secondary)
    34	
    35	`gitHead` is **NOT present** in the published tarballs for these versions
    36	(`grep gitHead package/package.json` returns nothing for any of the four).
    37	The GitHub commit SHAs below are resolved from the git tag of the form
    38	`@turnkey/<pkg>@<version>` via `gh api /repos/tkhq/sdk/git/ref/tags/...`.
    39	These are recorded for cross-reference only. The npm tarball sha256 above
    40	is the controlling wire-format source.
    41	
    42	| npm package                | Version | Git tag SHA                                  | Reference URL |
    43	|---|---|---|---|
    44	| @turnkey/encoding          | 0.6.0   | `60a997f4c52ac5f98bdd285af934f02699b88bff`   | https://github.com/tkhq/sdk/tree/60a997f4c52ac5f98bdd285af934f02699b88bff/packages/encoding |
    45	| @turnkey/crypto            | 2.8.8   | `b35dc642bd7c1728f97acd43d4cba66976b65084`   | https://github.com/tkhq/sdk/tree/b35dc642bd7c1728f97acd43d4cba66976b65084/packages/crypto |
    46	| @turnkey/api-key-stamper   | 0.5.0   | `b711befbb88ec522452dbdac68f0e98762be10dd`   | https://github.com/tkhq/sdk/tree/b711befbb88ec522452dbdac68f0e98762be10dd/packages/api-key-stamper |
    47	| @turnkey/http              | 3.16.0  | `8def9ba521233137437ac7294693a4ae0a0d14da`   | https://github.com/tkhq/sdk/tree/8def9ba521233137437ac7294693a4ae0a0d14da/packages/http |
    48	
    49	## Re-pinning procedure
    50	
    51	When bumping any pin:
    52	
    53	1. Update the table above (npm version, refresh tarball sha256, gitHead).
    54	2. Update `Directory.Packages.props` or any version comment.
    55	3. Re-run `npm pack` and refresh the extracted directories under
    56	   `upstream-snapshots/`.
    57	4. Re-run **3 Codex review rounds per affected file** via
    58	   [`codex-crypto-review.sh`](./codex-crypto-review.sh).
    59	5. Update `CHANGELOG.md`.

    51	
    52	test.each([
    53	  { impl: signNode, name: "stamp (node)" },
    54	  { impl: signWeb, name: "stamp (WebCrypto)" },
    55	  { impl: signUniversal, name: "stamp (universal)" },
    56	])("sign with openssl generated key pairs: $name", async ({ impl: stamp }) => {
    57	  // Run 20 times, where each run spawns 10 keys in parallel -> 200 tests in total
    58	  for (let i = 0; i < 20; i++) {
    59	    await Promise.all(
    60	      Array.from({ length: 10 }, () => true).map(async () => {
    61	        const { privateKey, publicKey, pemPublicKey } =
    62	          await generateKeyPairWithOpenSsl();
    63	
    64	        // A string of random unicode characters
    65	        const content = Array.from({ length: 64 }, () => {
    66	          return String.fromCharCode(Math.floor(Math.random() * 65536));
    67	        }).join("");
    68	
    69	        const signature = await stamp({
    70	          content,
    71	          privateKey,
    72	          publicKey,
    73	        });
    74	
    75	        // We can't snapshot `actualStamp.signature` because P-256 signatures are not deterministic
    76	        expect(
    77	          assertValidSignature({
    78	            content,
    79	            pemPublicKey,
    80	            signature: signature,
    81	          }),
    82	        ).toBe(true);
    83	
    84	        // Sanity check
    85	        expect(() => {
    86	          assertValidSignature({
    87	            content: "something else that wasn't stamped",
    88	            pemPublicKey,
    89	            signature: signature,
    90	          });
    91	        }).toThrow();
    92	      }),
    93	    );
    94	  }
    95	});

     7	
     8	test("pointDecode -> uncompressed invalid", async function () {
     9	  // Invalid uncompressed key (the last byte has been changed)
    10	  const uncompPubKey =
    11	    "0400d2eb47be2006c29db5fe9941dd686d19ddeea85a0328894f08091f6b5be9b366c4872345594c12a7f7c47c62dd8074542934820fce5ee0ddc55d6d1d8dd313";

    17	test("pointDecode -> uncompressed valid", async function () {
    18	  // Valid uncompressed public key with 00 as the first bit (test against 'x' field of JWK getting truncated)
    19	  const uncompPubKey =
    20	    "0400d2eb47be2006c29db5fe9941dd686d19ddeea85a0328894f08091f6b5be9b366c4872345594c12a7f7c47c62dd8074542934820fce5ee0ddc55d6d1d8dd312";
    21	  const jwk = pointDecode(uint8ArrayFromHexString(uncompPubKey));
    22	  expect(jwk.x).toBeDefined();
    23	  expect(jwk.y).toBeDefined();
    24	
    25	  // Convert x value to make sure it matches first half of uncompressed key WITHOUT truncating the first 0 bit
    26	  let xString: string = jwk.x !== undefined ? jwk.x : "_";
    27	  let xBytes = new Uint8Array(base64urlToBuffer(xString));
    28	  let xHex = uint8ArrayToHexString(xBytes);
    29	  expect(xHex).toBe(uncompPubKey.substring(2, 66));
    30	
    31	  // Convert y value to make sure it's the same as second half of uncompressed key
    32	  let yString: string = jwk.y !== undefined ? jwk.y : "_";
    33	  let yBytes = new Uint8Array(base64urlToBuffer(yString));
    34	  let yHex = uint8ArrayToHexString(yBytes);
    35	  expect(yHex).toBe(uncompPubKey.substring(66, 130));
    36	});

    38	test("pointDecode -> compressed", async function () {
    39	  // Valid compressed public key with 00 as the first bit (test against 'x' field of JWK getting truncated)
    40	  const uncompPubKey =
    41	    "0400d2eb47be2006c29db5fe9941dd686d19ddeea85a0328894f08091f6b5be9b366c4872345594c12a7f7c47c62dd8074542934820fce5ee0ddc55d6d1d8dd312";
    42	  // corresponding compressed public key
    43	  const compPubKey =
    44	    "0200d2eb47be2006c29db5fe9941dd686d19ddeea85a0328894f08091f6b5be9b3";
    45	
    46	  const jwk = pointDecode(uint8ArrayFromHexString(compPubKey));
    47	  expect(jwk.x).toBeDefined();
    48	  expect(jwk.y).toBeDefined();
    49	
    50	  // Convert x value to make sure it matches first half of uncompressed key WITHOUT truncating the first 0 bit
    51	  let xString: string = jwk.x !== undefined ? jwk.x : "_";
    52	  let xBytes = new Uint8Array(base64urlToBuffer(xString));
    53	  let xHex = uint8ArrayToHexString(xBytes);
    54	  expect(xHex).toBe(uncompPubKey.substring(2, 66));
    55	
    56	  // Convert y value to make sure it's the same as second half of uncompressed key
    57	  let yString: string = jwk.y !== undefined ? jwk.y : "_";
    58	  let yBytes = new Uint8Array(base64urlToBuffer(yString));
    59	  let yHex = uint8ArrayToHexString(yBytes);
    60	  expect(yHex).toBe(uncompPubKey.substring(66, 130));

    62	
    63	// Convert base64 url encoded string to an array -- used here to test that output pads correctly and doesn't get truncated
    64	function base64urlToBuffer(baseurl64String: string): ArrayBuffer {
    65	  // Base64url to Base64
    66	  const padding = "==".slice(0, (4 - (baseurl64String.length % 4)) % 4);
    67	  const base64String =
    68	    baseurl64String.replace(/-/g, "+").replace(/_/g, "/") + padding;
    69	
    70	  // Base64 to binary string
    71	  const str = atob(base64String);
    72	
    73	  // Binary string to buffer
    74	  const buffer = new ArrayBuffer(str.length);
    75	  const byteView = new Uint8Array(buffer);
    76	  for (let i = 0; i < str.length; i++) {
    77	    byteView[i] = str.charCodeAt(i);
    78	  }
    79	  return buffer;
    80	}
     1	import { test, expect } from "@jest/globals";
     2	import { ApiKeyStamper } from "../index";
     3	import { readFixture } from "../__fixtures__/shared";
     4	import { assertValidSignature } from "./shared";
     5	
     6	test("uses provided signature to make stamp", async function () {
     7	  const { privateKey, publicKey, pemPublicKey } = await readFixture();
     8	
     9	  const stamper = new ApiKeyStamper({
    10	    apiPublicKey: publicKey,
    11	    apiPrivateKey: privateKey,
    12	  });
    13	  const messageToSign = "hello from TKHQ!";
    14	  const stamp = await stamper.stamp(messageToSign);
    15	
    16	  expect(stamp.stampHeaderName).toBe("X-Stamp");
    17	
    18	  // We expect the stamp to be base64url encoded
    19	  const decodedStamp = JSON.parse(
    20	    Buffer.from(stamp.stampHeaderValue, "base64url").toString(),
    21	  );
    22	  // ...with 3 keys.
    23	  expect(Object.keys(decodedStamp)).toEqual([
    24	    "publicKey",
    25	    "scheme",
    26	    "signature",
    27	  ]);
    28	
    29	  // We assert against these keys one-by-one because P256 signatures aren't deterministic.
    30	  // Can't snapshot!
    31	  expect(decodedStamp["publicKey"]).toBe(publicKey);
    32	  expect(decodedStamp["scheme"]).toBe("SIGNATURE_SCHEME_TK_API_P256");
    33	  assertValidSignature({
    34	    content: messageToSign,
    35	    pemPublicKey: pemPublicKey,
    36	    signature: decodedStamp["signature"],
    37	  });
    38	});

    62	// Test for uint8ArrayFromHexString
    63	// Convert hex string to uint8 array
    64	test("uint8ArrayFromHexString", async function () {
    65	  const hexString =
    66	    "5234d08dfa2c815f3097b8ba848a28172e85bec78886e8e201afccb166fc54c1";
    67	  const expectedUint8Array = new Uint8Array([
    68	    82, 52, 208, 141, 250, 44, 129, 95, 48, 151, 184, 186, 132, 138, 40, 23, 46,
    69	    133, 190, 199, 136, 134, 232, 226, 1, 175, 204, 177, 102, 252, 84, 193,
    70	  ]);
    71	  expect(uint8ArrayFromHexString(hexString)).toEqual(expectedUint8Array); // Hex string => Uint8Array
    72	
    73	  expect(uint8ArrayFromHexString("627566666572").toString()).toEqual(
    74	    "98,117,102,102,101,114",
    75	  );
    76	
    77	  // Error case: empty string
    78	  expect(() => {
    79	    uint8ArrayFromHexString("");
    80	  }).toThrow("cannot create uint8array from invalid hex string");
    81	  // Error case: odd number of characters
    82	  expect(() => {
    83	    uint8ArrayFromHexString("123");
    84	  }).toThrow("cannot create uint8array from invalid hex string");
    85	  // Error case: bad characters outside of hex range
    86	  expect(() => {
    87	    uint8ArrayFromHexString("oops");
    88	  }).toThrow("cannot create uint8array from invalid hex string");
    89	  // Happy path: if length parameter is included, pad the resulting buffer
    90	  expect(uint8ArrayFromHexString("01", 2).toString()).toEqual("0,1");
    91	  // Happy path: if length parameter is omitted, do not pad the resulting buffer
    92	  expect(uint8ArrayFromHexString("01").toString()).toEqual("1");
    93	  // Error case: hex value cannot fit in desired length
    94	  expect(() => {
    95	    uint8ArrayFromHexString("0100", 1).toString(); // the number 256 cannot fit into 1 byte
    96	  }).toThrow("hex value cannot fit in a buffer of 1 byte(s)");
    97	
    98	  // TOO SHORT - test a hex string with less bytes than the "length" parameter provided
    99	  const hexString2 =
   100	    "5234d08dfa2c815f3097b8ba848a28172e85bec78886e8e201afccb166fc"; // length is 30 bytes, so must be padded with 2 0's at the beginning
   101	  const expectedUint8Array2 = new Uint8Array([
   102	    0, 0, 82, 52, 208, 141, 250, 44, 129, 95, 48, 151, 184, 186, 132, 138, 40,
   103	    23, 46, 133, 190, 199, 136, 134, 232, 226, 1, 175, 204, 177, 102, 252,
   104	  ]);
   105	  expect(uint8ArrayFromHexString(hexString2, 32)).toEqual(expectedUint8Array2); // Hex string => Uint8Array
   106	
   107	  // TOO LONG - test a hex string with less bytes than the "length" parameter provided -- Should error
   108	  const hexString3 =
   109	    "5234d08dfa2c815f3097b8ba848a28172e85bec78886e8e201afccb166fcfafbfcfd"; // length is 34 bytes, so no additional padding will be added
   110	  expect(() => uint8ArrayFromHexString(hexString3, 32)).toThrow(
   111	    "hex value cannot fit in a buffer of 32 byte(s)",
   112	  );
   113	});
   114	
   115	// Test for hexStringToBase64url
   116	test("hexStringToBase64url", async function () {
   117	  expect(hexStringToBase64url("01")).toEqual("AQ");
   118	  expect(hexStringToBase64url("01", 2)).toEqual("AAE");
   119	
   120	  // extrapolate to larger numbers

   123	
   124	  // error case
   125	  expect(() => {
   126	    hexStringToBase64url("0100", 1);
   127	  }).toThrow("hex value cannot fit in a buffer of 1 byte(s)");
   128	});

   225	        "activityStatus": "ACTIVITY_STATUS_FAILED",
